paper_title: Correlation between airborne pollen data and the risk of tick-borne encephalitis in northern Italy

methods.description: Process for collecting airborne pollen data using a Hirst-type sampler and standardizing the data for analysis.

steps.description: Pollen data were recorded at Fondazione Edmund Mach, in San Michele all' Adige, Province of Trento, Italy, from 1989 to 2020.
steps.searchable_summary: record pollen data from 1989 to 2020 at Fondazione Edmund Mach

steps.description: Airborne pollen was sampled by a Hirst-type sampler and analyzed following conventional techniques and standardized protocols (UNI EN 16868:2019).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze pollen samples using standardized protocols

steps.description: Standardize the pollen quantity collected during year y for each tree species T to standard scores by subtracting the mean and dividing by the standard deviation.

methods.description: Transform and model the data to investigate the association between pollen data and tick-borne encephalitis incidence.

steps.description: The annual TBE incidence I TBE (y) was transformed prior to analysis in order to normalize its distribution following the Box-Cox method.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Box-Cox transformation to TBE incidence data

steps.description: Develop univariate linear models to investigate the association between I TBE (y) * and the total amount of pollen of the tree species of interest collected during previous years.
steps.searchable_summary: create univariate linear models for pollen and TBE incidence

steps.description: Build a full model by considering all covariates having a significant slope in the univariate models, checking for potential collinearity.
steps.searchable_summary: construct full model with significant covariates and check for collinearity
